title: JWT (Backend)
objectives: Issue and validate JWTs on the server

sections.markdown: On the backend, JWTs are self-contained, signed tokens the server issues on login and validates on every request. Because they're stateless, you don't need a session store — but revocation is harder.

rows: Private key, verify with public key

sections.code:
Login:
  verify credentials -> issue access token (15 min) + refresh token (7 days)
On each request:
  parse Authorization: Bearer <token>
  verify signature + exp/iss/aud
  extract sub (userId) and scopes/roles
On refresh:
  validate refresh token -> rotate it -> issue new access token

sections.markdown: For multi-service architectures, RS256 wins: services validate with the public key from a JWKS endpoint — no shared secret to leak. Include `iss`, `aud`, `exp`, `nbf`, and a token id (`jti`) so you can revoke individual tokens.
Trusting the `alg` field from the token header — attackers can set `alg: none` or downgrade RS256 to HS256 and forge tokens. Pin the algorithm server-side and reject anything else.

Keep access tokens short (5–15 min), refresh tokens longer with rotation, and use a small `jti` blocklist in Redis for revocation. Sign with RS256/ES256 in production; publish keys via JWKS with rotation.

flashcards.back: HS256 uses a shared HMAC secret; RS256 uses a private key to sign + public key (JWKS) to verify — better for multi-service.

flashcards.front: How do you revoke a JWT?
flashcards.back: Short expiry + refresh rotation + optional jti blocklist in Redis.
alg:'none' or algorithm confusion; pin the expected algorithm server-side.

options: RS256 with a public key / JWKS
quiz.explain: Public-key verification avoids shared secrets across services.
Short lifetimes limit blast radius; refresh tokens renew.

quiz.q: The 'alg: none' attack is fixed by…
quiz.explain: Never trust the token's alg header; enforce it explicitly.

challenges.text: Issue and verify an HS256 JWT with exp/sub claims.
Switch to RS256 + JWKS endpoint; validate in a resource server.
Implement refresh-token rotation with a Redis-backed jti blocklist.
Add key rotation: publish 2 keys in JWKS, roll signing key without downtime.

interviewQuestions: What is JWKS and why use it?
How does the algorithm confusion attack work?
Access token vs refresh token vs ID token?
